facts.statement: Prologis is classified as a Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) and trades on the NYSE under the ticker PLD.
Prologis reported total revenue of approximately $8.02 billion for FY2025.
Prologis reported net income of approximately $3.06 billion for FY2025.
Prologis generated operating cash flow of approximately $5.37 billion for FY2025.
As of FY2025, Prologis had stockholders' equity of approximately $53.95 billion, long-term debt of approximately $30.88 billion, and cash and equivalents of approximately $530 million.
Prologis operates through two reportable segments: Real Estate Operations and Strategic Capital, with geographic operations spanning the United States, Other Americas, Europe, and Asia.

Prologis has operating properties and development projects across multiple countries including the US, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, the UK, France, Germany, Italy, Poland, Sweden, the Netherlands, Czech Republic, Hungary, Slovakia, Japan, China, and India.

Prologis has operating properties and development projects in numerous US markets including Southern California, the San Francisco Bay Area, Seattle, Nashville, Atlanta, Houston, Austin, Dallas, Denver, New Jersey/New York City, Lehigh Valley, Central Valley, Las Vegas, Reno, Cincinnati, Louisville, Charlotte, Baltimore, South Florida, Portland, and San Antonio.

Prologis manages multiple unconsolidated co-investment ventures including Prologis US Logistics Venture, Prologis European Logistics Partners Holding, Prologis European Logistics Fund, Prologis Japan Core Logistics Fund, Prologis China Logistics Venture, Prologis Brazil Logistics Venture, China AMC Prologis Logistics REIT, FIBRA, and Prologis Targeted US Logistics Fund.

Prologis maintains a diversified debt structure including senior notes in USD, EUR, GBP, and CAD, yen-denominated senior term loans, a global credit facility, commercial paper, secured mortgage debt, and term loans, with maturities extending to 2054.
Prologis entered into an Amended and Restated Global Senior Credit Agreement dated May 22, 2025, with Bank of America, N.A. as Global Administrative Agent.
Daniel S. Letter serves as Chief Executive Officer and Director of Prologis, Inc., signing the 10-K on February 13, 2026.
Timothy D. Arndt serves as Chief Financial Officer of Prologis, Inc.
Hamid R. Moghadam serves as Executive Chairman of Prologis, Inc.
Lori A. Palazzolo serves as Managing Director and Chief Accounting Officer, and Deborah K. Briones serves as Chief Legal Officer, General Counsel and Secretary of Prologis, Inc.
Prologis' Board is composed of 11 director nominees for the 2026 Annual Meeting, of which 82% (9 of 11) are independent, with only Hamid Moghadam and Daniel Letter being non-independent.

Prologis has adopted strong stockholder rights including annual election of all directors since IPO, majority vote standard for uncontested director elections, no stockholder rights plan, proxy access with 3/3/20/20 standard, and a reduced threshold for stockholders to call a special meeting of 20% of voting power adopted in 2025.
Prologis holds A2/A credit ratings from Moody's and S&P, respectively.
Prologis signed a company-record 228 million square feet of leases during 2025.
Prologis' annualized TSR since the AMB-ProLogis merger 14 years ago outperformed the MSCI U.S. REIT Index by 561 bps and the S&P 500 Equal Weighted Index by 102 bps per year.
Prologis' same store NOI on a net effective basis grew 4.5% for full year 2025, with same store portfolio NOI of approximately $8.95 billion on approximately 1,125 million square feet.

As of year-end 2025, Prologis had a gross market capitalization of approximately $160.9 billion, total Prologis share of debt net of adjustments of approximately $39.5 billion, and debt as a percentage of gross market capitalization of 24.6%.
KPMG LLP serves as Prologis' independent registered public accounting firm, with the Audit Committee recommending ratification of its appointment for 2026.

Prologis' compensation program includes annual bonus opportunities, long-term incentive equity awards, and the Prologis Promote Plan (PPP) as outperformance compensation, with forward-looking PSU awards incentivizing relative TSR outperformance.

Prologis has formal Board oversight of Global Impact & Sustainability performance through the Governance Committee, with a chief energy and sustainability officer who sits on the management executive committee, and sustainability metrics comprising 10% of the overall corporate bonus scorecard.
